message:
feat(core): add ARS FST interception engine and per-pane trauma guard

Introduces two new modules that wire the ARS (Autonomous Recovery System)
into the terminal's hot path:

ars_intercept.rs (1009 lines):
- FST-based interception of PTY error events in the terminal pipeline
- When an error regime is detected (via BOCPD/MinHash), queries the compiled
  FST for matching reflexes before falling through to the expensive LLM loop
- Context gate validates pane environment (CWD, env vars, shell) matches the
  reflex's required bounds before executing — graceful LLM fallback on mismatch
- Decision flow: PTY output → Error Detected → MinHash/Trigger → FST Lookup →
  {No Match | Context Fail → LLM Fallback} or {Match OK → Execute Reflex}

trauma_guard.rs (512 lines):
- Per-pane trauma state tracking for deterministic failure loop detection
- Bloom filter for fast "have we seen this error signature recently?" checks
- Time-windowed recurrence accounting via SlidingWindow
- Bounded in-memory history for explainability and debugging
- Configurable loop threshold triggers intervention when repeated failures
  exceed the threshold within the sliding window

Integration (ingest.rs + lib.rs):
- PaneRegistry now tracks TraumaState per pane alongside existing cursors
- States are created on pane registration and cleaned up on removal
- New accessor methods (get_trauma_state, get_trauma_state_mut) for the
  control plane to query and update trauma state
- ars_intercept module registered in lib.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
